text:
Skye Nicolson, the WBC women’s world featherweight champion, has hit out at the ‘horrible’ treatment towards Imane Khelif and Lin Yu-ting at the Olympics and has branded Angela Carini’s decision to withdraw from her fight as a ‘publicity stunt’.

Controversy has surrounded the participation of Khelif and Lin at the Olympics in Paris this summer after they were disqualified from the Women’s World Championships last year for failing International Boxing Association (IBA) eligibility rules which prevents athletes with XY chromosomes from participating in women’s events.

The IBA has not specified why Khelif and Lin failed their gender eligibility tests but has continued to criticise the International Olympic Committee’s (IOC) decision to allow both fighters to compete in the women’s division in Paris.

Both Khelif and Lin competed in the women’s division at the Tokyo Olympics three years agoand have always been identified as female since birth.

Khelif is now guaranteed to win the first Olympic medal of her career after she beat Hungary’s Anna Luca Hamori in her 66kg quarter final on Saturday, while Lin will fight Bulgaria’s Svetlana Staneva in the 57kg quarter final at 10am on Sunday.

Nicolson, meanwhile, has lost twice to Taiwan’s Lin and has also fought and trained with Algeria’s Khelif, and points out that both fighters have lost to female opponents on multiple occasions.

‘I’ve actually fought and sparred both of the girls – they were born female,’ Nicolson said in a video on Instagram.

‘They were born with an XY chromosome, which is the male chromosome, but they were born with female bodies – they have the physical attributes of a female.

‘They have grown up as girls, as women. They have competed as women the whole time. These are not naturally born men who have decided to call themselves women or identify as women to fight women in the Olympics.

‘So when the IBA decided to do a chromosome test at the World Championships, it was discovered that these two athletes had XY chromosomes.

‘It wasn’t a testosterone test, it wasn’t like they didn’t pass something like that. It wasn’t a physical test, they don’t have the physical attributes of a male.

Differences of Sexual Development are a group of rare conditions involving genes, hormones and reproductive organs. Some people with DSDs are raised as female but have XY sex chromosomes and blood testosterone levels in the male range.

‘And while it is a bit of a grey area, I think the abuse and the power of the media, and people just jumping on the bandwagon without knowing all the details, is honestly horrible.

‘These girls have represented their country on numerous occasions for many, many years now, as female fighters, and they do not deserve this mistreatment. I think they have been so unfortunate enough to even be in the situation that they’re in and to get all this scrutiny as well.

‘These girls have been beat by girls, numerous times, both of them. If they were men and it was dangerous for girls to be fighting them they would be winning gold at everything – they’re not. I think that says it all.’

Nicolson also criticised Carini,who has since offered an apology to Khelif after refusing to shake her handfollowing their fight which lasted just 46 seconds on Thursday.

Carini, who was left in tears in the ring, complained to her coach that the fight was ‘not fair’ before she decided to abandon the contest in the first round.

‘I feel like the thing that happened with the Italian girl was a publicity stunt more than anything,’ Nicolson continued.

‘And I just think people really need to, like, look at the facts and stop jumping on the bandwagon. Be a lion, not a sheep.’

title: Imane Khelif: Aussie boxing champion blasts Angela Carini for Olympics ‘publicity stunt’ | Metro News
description: Skye Nicolson, the WBC world featherweight champ, hits out at the 'horrible' treatment towards Imane Khelif and Lin Yu-ting at the Olympics
